source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An electronics switch \( S \) is required to block voltage of either polarity during its OFF state as shown in the figure (a) of <image>. This switch is required to conduct in only one direction in its ON state as shown in the figure(b) of <image>.
Which of the following are valid realizations of the switch \( S \)?
extraction_notes: image field is present as a PIL image object representation, but no image path or URL is provided

issues: metadata.image_paths is empty and metadata.extraction_notes says no image path or URL was provided, which is inconsistent with the top-level image_paths field.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to figure (a) and figure (b)
the validity of realizations P/Q/R/S depends on circuit diagrams not described in text
multi_step: question imposes at least two distinct constraints: block either-polarity voltage in OFF state and conduct only one direction in ON state
solver must evaluate multiple candidate realizations (P, Q, R, S) against these constraints
multiple-choice options combine candidates, requiring comparison and filtering rather than direct lookup
question_text is conceptual and diagram-based, suggesting nontrivial circuit reasoning
verifiability: choices are provided and answer_text='P and R' matches choice C exactly
answer is a discrete, unambiguous option set
the required properties for each realization are rule-checkable from the figure

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, one existing image, and an answer are all available.
The sample appears strongly image-dependent, and the required image asset exists, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scoring is strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability.
